Scenario.title: RedDelta Modified PlugX Attack Scenario
Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple This scenario depicts a targeted attack against water and wastewater infrastructure leveraging expert-level skills in phishing and exploitation, resulting in operational disruption through manipulated control settings.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Email Gateway, Web Servers, Remote Access Points
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Spear Phishing, Social Engineering, Malicious Payload Delivery

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: An adversary initiates contact through spear-phishing emails directed at personnel responsible for managing internet-connected systems, such as SCADA interfaces or administrative portals. These emails contain deceptive links or attachments designed to harvest authentication credentials when accessed.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-9: Buffer Overflow in Local Command-Line Utilities
stage.description: Upon obtaining valid credentials via phishing, the attacker leverages existing remote access points or exploits vulnerabilities in command interfaces to execute arbitrary code or commands across the networked systems controlling water treatment facilities.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-166: Force the System to Reset Values
stage.description: The attacker manipulates key OS processes associated with SCADA or control system components to alter critical parameters, such as pump speeds or valve configurations, leading to service disruptions or unintended operational states.

Scenario.summary: This attack chain demonstrates a focused compromise of water and wastewater systems starting with credential theft via targeted phishing, escalating privileges through command injection techniques, and culminating in deliberate manipulation of control systems to cause operational interference.
